summary: The resistance of a uniform wire depends on its material and shape: R = ρ·L/S. Longer wires resist more; thicker wires resist less.
method: Identify the wire's material (its resistivity ρ), its length L, and its cross-sectional area S.
Decide which quantity is unknown — resistance, resistivity, length, or area.
Rearrange R = ρ·L/S for the unknown.
Use SI units (Ω·m, m, m²); for a round wire, S = π·r².
Substitute and compute; check that longer/thinner gives more resistance.

example.title: Resistance of a copper wire
steps: The unknown is R, so use R = ρ·L/S.
